stem: Two office buildings stand on flat ground. Building A is 60 feet tall and Building B is 24 feet tall. A straight enclosed walkway connects the rooftop of Building B to the rooftop of Building A, making a 53-degree angle with the horizontal. The walkway has glass panels on both sides and the buildings share underground parking. Using the approximations sin(53) = 4/5 and cos(53) = 3/5, what is the length of the walkway, in feet?
explanation: The underground parking is extra information that should be ignored. The walkway rises from the top of Building B (24 feet) to the top of Building A (60 feet), so the vertical rise is 60 - 24 = 36 feet. The walkway forms the hypotenuse of a right triangle with the vertical rise as the opposite side. Using sin(53) = opposite/hypotenuse: 4/5 = 36/walkway, so walkway = 36 * (5/4) = 45 feet.

distractorRationale.A: Computed the horizontal distance between the buildings instead of the walkway length: horizontal = 36/tan(53) = 36/(4/3) = 27
distractorRationale.B: Used the full height of Building A (60 feet) as the vertical rise instead of the height difference (60 - 24 = 36): walkway = 60/sin(53) = 60/(4/5) = 75
distractorRationale.D: Used cos(53) instead of sin(53) to find the walkway length: walkway = 36/cos(53) = 36/(3/5) = 60

imageDescription: Two vertical rectangles represent the buildings on flat ground. Building A on the left is taller (labeled 60 ft) and Building B on the right is shorter (labeled 24 ft). A diagonal line connects the rooftops, representing the walkway. The angle between the walkway and a horizontal dashed line at Building B's rooftop level is labeled 53 degrees. The vertical difference between the rooftops (36 ft) is indicated with a bracket on the left side.
